problem_statement: Addresses the lack of clear definitions, influencing factors, and metrics for trust/distrust/trustworthiness in LLMs for software engineering, where misaligned trust (overtrust or undertrust) in AI-generated code undermines effective and safe human oversight.
magnitude_evidence: up to 7% of automatic refactorings broke functionality or introduced syntax errors [67] (ChatGPT/Gemini Java study, demonstrating risks of overtrust)
insufficiency_evidence: when LLM-generated suggestions contained subtle security vulnerabilities, developers were more likely to trust and use them without adequate scrutiny, leading to increased security risks [86]
solution_mechanisms: manual code review of LLM-generated code
key_constructs: distrust as active expectation of failure
notes: Body partially truncated ('[...TRUNCATED FOR LENGTH...]') before conclusion.
